title: eriosorus flabellatus
intro: Eriosorus flabellatus, also known as the New Zealand fern-leaved lingualaria, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the subalpine regions of New Zealand. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of Eriosorus flabellatus include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or by using a tincture to create a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds.
